question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a rogue mirror node endpoint (via misconfigured MonitorProperties) so that grpcClient.subscribe(subscription) streams fabricated TopicMessage records with forged consensusTimestamp values, causing the monitor to record falsified Hashgraph history without any integrity check?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to return a non-INVALID_ARGUMENT gRPC error repeatedly so that shouldRetry() always returns true, triggering infinite retries via Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, ...) and masking a gap in Hashgraph message history by preventing the subscriber from ever advancing past the tampered sequence point?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the topicId property (via expressionConverter.convert()) to redirect the GrpcSubscription to a different topic, causing the monitor to subscribe to attacker-controlled topic history instead of the legitimate Hashgraph topic, thereby replacing authentic consensus history with fabricated messages?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.defer(this::createSubscriptions).cache() pattern so that once a tampered GrpcSubscription is cached, all subsequent calls to subscribe() return the poisoned subscription indefinitely, permanently corrupting the monitor's view of Hashgraph history for the lifetime of the process?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a gRPC response stream that delivers TopicMessages with out-of-order or duplicate sequenceNumbers, knowing that GrpcSubscription.onNext() only logs a warning (log.warn) for sequence gaps without rejecting or flagging the message, causing the monitor to silently accept tampered Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of TLS/mTLS enforcement on the gRPC channel (no certificate pinning visible in GrpcClientSDK) to perform a man-in-the-middle attack, substituting legitimate TopicMessage payloads with forged ones containing manipulated consensusTimestamp values that alter the perceived Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the cached Flux<GrpcSubscription> (Flux.defer(this::createSubscriptions).cache()) to observe that once a subscription pointing to a tampered topic is established, getSubscriptions() will always return the poisoned subscription, making it impossible for the system to self-correct without a full restart?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the expressionConverter.convert(properties.getTopicId()) call to inject a topic expression (e.g., ${topic.malicious}) that resolves to an attacker-controlled topic ID, causing the monitor to subscribe to and validate fabricated consensus messages as legitimate Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user send a stream of TopicMessages where the contents field contains a crafted timestamp (parsed by Utility.getTimestamp(topicMessage.contents)) that is far in the past or future, causing the monitor to compute incorrect publishedTimestamp values and report falsified latency/history metrics for Hashgraph consensus events?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to return UNKNOWN status codes (getStatusCode returns Status.Code.UNKNOWN for non-StatusRuntimeException throwables) so that shouldRetry() always returns true, enabling an attacker to stall the subscriber indefinitely at a specific sequence number and create an artificial gap in the observed Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doBeforeRetry log statement (StringUtils.substringBefore(r.failure().getMessage(), '\n')) to inject newline-delimited log entries via a crafted gRPC error message, poisoning audit logs that record Hashgraph subscription history and obscuring evidence of tampering?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the properties.setTopicId(topicId) mutation (which permanently overwrites the topicId on the shared GrpcSubscriberProperties object) to cause all subsequent GrpcSubscription instances created from the same properties to subscribe to a tampered topic, affecting all subscribers in the loop (for i = 1 to properties.getSubscribers())?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the retry.setMaxAttempts(Long.MAX_VALUE) default in GrpcSubscriberProperties to cause the monitor to retry indefinitely against a rogue mirror node that serves manipulated Hashgraph history, with no circuit breaker or maximum retry cap to fall back to a trusted source?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the flatMap(this::clientSubscribe) call to cause concurrent subscriptions to the same topic to receive divergent message streams (e.g., one stream with tampered history, one with legitimate), and since there is no cross-subscription consistency check, the monitor will silently accept both without detecting the discrepancy?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getStatusCode()] Can an unprivileged external user wrap a tampered-history error in a non-StatusRuntimeException throwable so that getStatusCode() returns Status.Code.UNKNOWN, bypassing the INVALID_ARGUMENT check in shouldRetry() and causing the subscriber to retry indefinitely against a mirror node serving falsified consensus data?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of message authentication (no HMAC, signature, or Merkle proof verification on TopicMessage payloads) in the clientSubscribe() pipeline to substitute legitimate consensus messages with forged ones, and have them accepted as valid Hashgraph history by the monitor?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that subscribeProperties.getGrpc().values() iterates over all configured gRPC subscriber properties without any runtime integrity check on topicId values, allowing a tampered configuration (e.g., via environment variable injection) to redirect all subscriptions to attacker-controlled topics serving fake Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Retry.backoff() configuration (minBackoff, maxBackoff from RetryProperties) to time attacks precisely — by causing transient errors during legitimate message windows and forcing retries that skip over authentic consensus messages, creating artificial gaps in the Hashgraph history observed by the monitor?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reactive Flux pipeline (subscriptions.flatMap(this::clientSubscribe)) to cause backpressure starvation — by flooding the sink with high-volume fabricated TopicMessages — overwhelming the parallel scheduler and causing legitimate messages to be dropped, effectively erasing portions of authentic Hashgraph history from the monitor's view?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doOnError(t -> log.error('Error subscribing {}:', subscription, t)) handler to leak internal subscription state (topic IDs, sequence numbers, timestamps) via error messages, enabling reconnaissance of the Hashgraph history monitoring configuration to plan a more targeted tampering attack?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the GrpcSubscription constructor (new GrpcSubscription(i, properties)) which shares the same GrpcSubscriberProperties instance across all subscriber instances (i=1 to subscribers), so that mutating properties.setTopicId() after construction affects all subscribers simultaneously, redirecting all of them to a tampered Hashgraph topic?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Sinks.many().multicast().directBestEffort() sink in GrpcClientSDK.subscribeToClient() to cause message drops under load (directBestEffort does not guarantee delivery), resulting in the monitor silently missing legitimate consensus messages and creating undetected gaps in the Hashgraph history record?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the binary retry logic (only INVALID_ARGUMENT is not retried) to cause the monitor to retry indefinitely on a mirror node that serves a forked/tampered version of Hashgraph history, since the tampered node would return valid gRPC status codes (OK or UNAVAILABLE) that pass the shouldRetry() filter?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of topicId format validation beyond @NotBlank in GrpcSubscriberProperties to supply a malformed or adversarial topic ID string that, when passed to TopicId.fromString() in GrpcSubscription.getTopicMessageQuery(), resolves to an unintended topic containing fabricated Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the topicMessageQuery.setMaxAttempts(0) setting (which disables SDK-level retries) combined with a rogue mirror node that terminates connections immediately after delivering tampered messages, forcing the custom retry logic in clientSubscribe() to reconnect and re-receive the same forged messages repeatedly, amplifying the impact of Hashgraph history manipulation?
